mechanism: The Phillips Curve slope and inflation expectations anchoring interact dynamically: anchored expectations flatten the curve, while a steep curve puts more pressure on expectations to de-anchor under supply shocks, creating a potential feedback loop at the boundary.
regime_dependence: Most critical at the boundary of anchoring (5Y breakevens 2.3-2.5%). If expectations de-anchor, the curve steepens, which puts further pressure on expectations — a potential nonlinear transition.
